findings.title: Harness does not acknowledge clean first-launch brief input
findings.detail: `execAction` counts `ActDeliverInput` only when it is a resume prompt and never feeds a clean `EvInputAck` for the normal `InputBrief` path, despite the file header saying the harness answers Run-machine port actions with clean substrate results. Non-resumed schedules therefore leave Dispatch stuck in `Briefing`; later `outcome_received`/`heartbeat_stale` stimuli are ignored by Dispatch, and the pump eventually converts the cell into `input_undeliverable`/reopen. Because the matrix accepts any terminal, successful clean strata such as `dot` can pass without exercising the intended completed path.
findings.suggested_fix: Have the harness acknowledge every clean `ActDeliverInput` with a correlated `EvInputAck` unless the specific fault cell is intentionally withholding that ack, and tighten clean-cell assertions to match the corpus summary outcome.

findings.title: Injected events after a terminal are discarded
findings.detail: The drive loop breaks as soon as the Run machine reaches Done, even when `deliverableEvents` says the Twin will still emit an injected duplicate or disconnect sentinel. `feedStep` also returns early once the run is no longer in flight. As a result, `FaultDup` at a terminal-producing stimulus does not actually feed the duplicate through the reactors, so the advertised every-position idempotence coverage is weaker than the matrix claims.
findings.suggested_fix: Continue draining the calculated deliverable count through the harness and assert that post-terminal stimuli produce no additional terminal actions, or explicitly exclude terminal-position duplicate/drop cells from the advertised matrix.
